data.title: Caledonia Mining guides lower FY26 gold production from Blanket mine, flags higher costs
data.description:
Caledonia Mining (CMCL) expects to produce 72,000-76,500 ounces of gold from its Blanket Mine in Zimbabwe in FY 2026, with the quarterly production profile expected to be stronger in the second half of the year as higher-grade areas increasingly come on stream.

Total group capital expenditure is projected to be US$162.5M. Cost guidance for the year reflects higher direct operating costs at the mine compared with the first nine months of 2025, primarily due to inflation and increased costs in mining, milling and processing, and engineering departments.

Blanket gold production totaled 76,213 ounces in FY 2025, meeting the company’s increased guidance range of 75,500 to 79,500 ounces and broadly in line with output from the previous two years. Fourth-quarter 2025 production came in at 17,367 ounces, down from 19,841 ounces in Q4 2024.

Production in the second half of 2025 was adversely affected by lower tonnages from higher-grade areas (which is being addressed) and interruptions in the electricity supply at the end of the quarter.

Separately, Caledonia Mining (CMCL) said James Mufara, chief operating officer, has left the company. The company confirms that its operations continue to be managed by the existing executive and operating team, and the operations and strategy remain unchanged. It does not currently intend to appoint another COO.

data.title: J4 Ventures Resources announces CFO transition
Metasphere Labs announces private placement
data.description:
• Metasphere Labs (LABZF) announced a proposed non-brokered private placement to raise upto $600K through the issuance of up to 12M units at a price of $0.05 per unit.

  • Each unit will consist of one common share in the capital of the company and one transferable common share purchase warrant.

  • Each warrant will entitle the holder to acquire one additional common share at a price of $0.06 for a period of four years from the closing date.

  • The proceeds will be used for general working capital and allocation of funds toward general corporate purposes.

  • The offering is expected to closed on or about January 23, 2026.

data.title: Musk says Tesla FSD only available as subscription after Feb. 14
data.description:
• Tesla (TSLA) will stop selling full self-driving after Feb. 14, CEO Elon Musk said in a post on X.
  • “FSD will only be available as a monthly subscription thereafter,” Musk wrote.

data.title: Antero Resources prices $750 million senior notes offering
data.description:
• Antero Resources (AR) on Tuesday announced the pricing of an underwritten public offering of $750 million in aggregate principal amount of 5.40% senior unsecured notes due 2036 at an initial price to the public of 99.869%.
  • The offering is expected to close on January 28, 2026.
  • Antero Resources (AR) estimates that it will receive net proceeds of approximately $743 million and intends to use the same to partially fund the HG Acquisition.

data.title: U.S. formally clears Nvidia H200 AI chip exports to China with some conditions
data.description:
The Trump administration on Tuesday formally approved sales of Nvidia’s second-most powerful AI chips to China, establishing a new regulatory framework that could pave the way for shipments of the H200 despite strong opposition from China hawks in Washington.

Under the rules, the chips must be reviewed by an third-party testing lab to verify their AI capabilities before export. Shipments to China are capped at no more than 50% of the volume sold to U.S. customers.

Nvidia (NVDA) will also be required to certify that sufficient supplies of the H200 remain available in the U.S., while Chinese buyers must demonstrate "sufficient security procedures" and certify that the chips will not be used for military purposes—conditions that had not previously been defined.

Responding to Seeking Alpha’s request for comment, an Nvidia spokesperson said the policy set by U.S. President Donald Trump “strikes a thoughtful balance that is great for America” and would help the company remain competitive in the global semiconductor market.

"The Administration's critics are unintentionally promoting the interests of foreign competitors on U.S. entity lists—America should always want its industry to compete for vetted and approved commercial business, supporting real jobs for real Americans.”

The H200 is an older-generation processor cleared for export by the Trump administration, while sales of Nvidia’s most advanced chips remain restricted on national security grounds. In December, President Donald Trump approved the sale of some advanced AI chips, including Nvidia's H200 GPUs, to China in exchange for a 25% surcharge, opening a path for Nvidia (NVDA) to recover some lost revenue.

The Information reported on Tuesday that China had approved tech companies purchasing H200 GPUs in some cases. The Chinese government told some local companies that H200 AI accelerators could be purchased under special circumstances, citing examples such as university research or development labs, the news outlet added, citing two people with direct knowledge of the situation.

The missive from Chinese government officials was also vague, as they told the companies to buy the products if it was “necessary,” but did not specify what necessary meant, the news outlet added.

Nvidia (NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang said earlier this month that he did not believe the Chinese government will make a formal declaration that it has allowed Chinese companies to import the chipmaker's H200 GPUs. Instead, the proof would come through purchase orders.

ByteDance (BDNCE) and Alibaba (BABA) are among the Chinese companies thinking about ordering H200s from Nvidia, which reportedly has been in contact with Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) about increasing production.

data.title: SEALSQ eyes investment and possible acquisition of Quobly
data.description:
• SEALSQ Corp (LAES) on Wednesday said that it has signed a non-binding agreement to start exclusive talks with the shareholders of Quobly SAS, a French company developing silicon-based quantum computers.
  • The plan is to make an initial minority investment, with the possibility of later acquiring a majority stake.
  • The deal still depends on final agreements, due diligence, regulatory approvals, and other standard closing steps.
  • This transaction is anchored within SEALSQ’s Quantum strategy and supported in part by its dedicated Quantum Fund designed to accelerate the emergence of sovereign, secure, and industrial-scale quantum technologies across Europe.
  • If completed, the transaction would result in a total investment of approximately $200M by SEALSQ (LAES) in return for a majority stake in QUOBLY.

data.title: BrianCell Therapeutics drops 10%, prices $30M offering at $5.59 per unit
data.description:
• BriaCell Therapeutics (BCTX) priced a public offering of 5.37M units, with each unit consisting of one common share and one warrant.
  • Each unit is being sold to the public at a price of $5.59 per unit for gross proceeds of ~$30M.
  • The offering is expected to close on January 15, 2026.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds to fund working capital requirements, general corporate purposes and the advancement of the company’s business objectives.
  • The stock price dropped 9.7% on Tuesday during after-market hours of trading.

data.title: Amazon seeks discounts from suppliers to limit tariff shock - report
data.description:
E-commerce giant Amazon (AMZN) is negotiating with suppliers to reduce payments for the goods it sells on the platform to offset U.S. tariffs under President Donald Trump.

The tech company is attempting to transfer risk of additional trade volatility to its suppliers by asking them to agree to pay any levies associated with the goods they sell, the Financial Times reported, citing sources familiar with the matter.

“We work closely with vendors to understand all the cost pressures they’re facing — tariffs, supply chain, raw materials, labour — and factor those into negotiations,” Amazon said, according to the report.

Amazon is seeking discounts from low single digits to as high as 30%, according to several vendor consultants. The company has engaged in talks and given a January 1 deadline in some cases and wants suppliers to cover future duties, marketing, and promotions for smaller discounts.

data.title: TCS and AMD team up to accelerate AI adoption
data.description:
• India's Tata Consultancy Services announced a strategic collaboration with AMD (AMD) to help enterprises scale AI adoption from pilots to production, modernize legacy environments, and build secure, high-performance digital workplaces.
  • Under this partnership, TCS and AMD will work together to develop AI and GenAI solutions for specific industries, combining TCS’s expertise and global network with AMD’s high-performance computing and AI products.
  • The two companies plan to jointly invest in talent that will help build a deep pool of experts who can co-innovate and deliver next-generation AI solutions.
